title: 5 takeaways from Brad Stevens’s end of the year press conference
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics "One of the things we've got to figure out is how to have more of an impact at the rim and I think we do need to add to our team to do that.
" Brad Stevens speaking at his end-of-season press conference on May 6, 2026.
David L Ryan/Globe Staff ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Apartment availability in Boston climbs to the highest level in years
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News More units, shifting student demand, and economic uncertainty are reshaping the rental market, with monthly prices still climbing.
An apartment building in Brighton.
Taylor Coester for The Boston Globe ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Watch: Wellesley mother who allegedly killed her children appears in Mass. court on murder charges
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Janette MacAusland allegedly killed her children at home in Wellesley amid a contentious divorce and custody battle.
Livestream via NBC10 Boston.
Janette MacAusland, the Wellesley woman accused of killing her two children amid a contentious divorce and custody battle, will appear in court Wednesday to face murder charges.

title: Rescued, underweight Cane Corso mix to be ready for adoption this weekend after swift recovery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Balboa will be ready for his forever home after getting neutered Friday.
Balboa, a Cane Corso mix rescued in Dorchester, was brought in to MSPCA-Angell's emergency room (left) and has since been recovering and gaining weight (right).
Courtesy of MSPCA-Angell A then-severely underweight stray dog rescued in Dorchester is expected to be ready for adoption this weekend, significantly sooner than originally anticipated by caretakers.

title: Tracy McGrady: Jaylen Brown has deep frustration with Celtics organization
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics "There's just been a lot of stuff that I've been hearing going on with the Boston organization with JB.
" Jaylen Brown during the Celtics' playoff loss to the 76ers.
Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff) Jaylen Brown has been outspoken about some of his frustrations in the aftermath of the Celtics first-round loss to the 76ers.

title: The Sagamore Bridge replacement project is underway – sort of
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News MassDOT has started its foundation load testing program to help make way for the new bridge.
The Sagamore Bridge spans the Cape Cod Canal.
JohnTlumacki/Globe Staff (metro) If you’ve spotted that giant crane looming beside the Sagamore Bridge, don’t get too excited.

title: This Mass. spot ranked among America’s ‘dreamiest’ beach camping escapes
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: New England Travel You'll find it on the Cape.
Race Point Beach in Provincetown.
Flickr / Massachusetts Office of Travel & Tourism Travelers can combine their love of camping and the beach for a “dreamy” escape on Cape Cod this summer, according to Outside magazine.

title: Mass. collects nearly $7.4 billion in tax revenue in April, surpassing projections by almost $1 billion
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News The state collected 7.
7 percent more in tax revenue this April than in April 2025, officials said.
The Massachusetts State House in Boston.

title: Why 1,100 Scotland World Cup fans are taking school buses to Foxborough
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: World Cup Priced out of Boston, Tartan Army supporters organized buses from Rhode Island — cutting travel costs in half.
Gregor Cowan (center, yellow sunglasses), is an organizer with the Tartan Army, which is sending 1,100 Scotland soccer fans to Gillette Stadium on yellow school buses for the 2026 World Cup.
(Photo courtesy of Gregor Cowan) By Annie Jonas May 6, 2026 | 11:18 AM 3 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Police search for suspect accused of stealing wine bottle, hitting victim in head
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime The incident happened Friday afternoon.
Photos of the suspect who police say stole a wine bottle and then struck a victim.
Courtesy of Boston Police Department ShareSend this article to your social connections.
